summary: binding.gyp command-expansion executes shell at npm install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the package root containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) within the `targets`/`sources` fields. npm implicitly invokes `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a `binding.gyp` is present — even with no declared install/postinstall script — and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during the configure step. As a result, the embedded command runs automatically on `npm install`, functionally equivalent to a lifecycle hook. The package presents itself as a Backstage LDAP auth backend plugin (pure TypeScript/Node code), and there is no legitimate need for a native build descriptor or command-expansion in a binding.gyp for that purpose. This pattern allows arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
